name: Employees' State Insurance Act, 1948
officialCitation: The Employees' State Insurance Act, 1948 (Act No. 34 of 1948), repealed and saved under section 164 of the Code on Social Security, 2020.
overview: Historical central labour-law record governing employees' state insurance coverage, contributions and benefits before the Code on Social Security, 2020 became operative on 21 November 2025. The Employees' State Insurance Act, 1948 was repealed by section 164 of the Code, subject to statutory savings. Current employees' state insurance obligations must be assessed under Chapter IV of the Code on Social Security, 2020, together with applicable rules, regulations, schemes, notifications and ESIC directions. Rules, regulations and schemes made under the repealed Act remain in force, to the extent they are not inconsistent with the Code, for the one-year savings period specified in section 164. This record is retained for historical periods, saved instruments, exemptions, claims, proceedings and audit traceability.

definitions.definition: An employee covered under the Employees' State Insurance Act and the applicable scheme.
The establishment or person responsible for compliance under the Act.
An employee registered under the Employees' State Insurance Scheme.

rules.title: Review ESI Applicability
rules.description: Determine whether the establishment is required to register under the Employees' State Insurance Scheme based on applicable legal criteria and notified coverage.

rules.title: Employer Registration
rules.description: Register the establishment with ESIC where coverage applies.

rules.title: Employee Registration
rules.description: Register eligible employees under the Employees' State Insurance Scheme.

rules.title: Monthly ESI Contributions
rules.description: Deposit employer and employee contributions within the prescribed timelines at the applicable contribution rates.

rules.title: Maintain ESI Records
rules.description: Maintain statutory registers, wage records and contribution records required under the scheme.

rules.title: Facilitate Statutory Benefits
rules.description: Ensure eligible employees are able to access statutory medical and cash benefits available under the ESI Scheme.

rules.title: Apply Repeal and Savings Requirements
rules.description: Treat the Employees' State Insurance Act, 1948 as repealed from 21 November 2025 and use Chapter IV of the Code on Social Security, 2020 for current compliance. Continue to apply rules, regulations, schemes, notifications, benefits, appointments, orders and actions under the repealed Act only to the extent preserved by section 164, including the specific one-year continuation of ESI rules, regulations and schemes where they are not inconsistent with the Code.

citation.reference: Implementation notification for the Code on Social Security, 2020

exceptions.title: Coverage depends on notified applicability
exceptions.description: Applicability varies based on notified establishments, employee coverage criteria and other legal provisions. The engine should evaluate these before recommending compliance actions.

exceptions.title: Saved ESI Rules, Regulations and Schemes
exceptions.description: Rules, regulations and schemes made under the Employees' State Insurance Act, 1948 remain in force, to the extent they are not inconsistent with the Code on Social Security, 2020, for one year from the commencement of the Code. Other saved notifications, benefits, orders and actions continue only as provided by section 164.

recommendations.title: Retain Historical ESI Records
recommendations.description: Preserve establishment registration, insured-person, wage, contribution, accident, benefit, exemption, assessment and proceeding records created under the repealed Act for saved liabilities and audit traceability.

recommendations.title: Use the Social Security Code Record for Current Compliance
recommendations.description: Assess current employees' state insurance obligations under CENTRAL-SOCIAL-SECURITY while continuing to follow saved ESIC rules, regulations, schemes, notifications and operational directions during the statutory transition.

sources.name: Code on Social Security, 2020 and Employees' State Insurance Act, 1948
sources.authority: Legislative Department, Ministry of Law and Justice, Government of India

sources.name: Ministry of Labour & Employment Labour Codes Portal
sources.authority: Ministry of Labour & Employment, Government of India

governance.changeSummary: Updated the Employees' State Insurance Act record as a superseded historical record, preserved its existing definitions, rule identifiers and coverage exception, documented the statutory savings for ESI rules, regulations and schemes, and linked current employees' state insurance compliance to the Code on Social Security, 2020 from 21 November 2025.
